source_policy: Weather-type labels must cite CWA weather maps, warnings, daily reports, or official open-data products. Radar-only evidence is not enough.

official_source_candidates.name: CWA weather maps
official_source_candidates.use: Synoptic pattern: front, Mei-yu front, typhoon circulation, or local convection context.

official_source_candidates.name: CWA weather warnings and advisories
official_source_candidates.use: Heavy-rain, extremely-heavy-rain, thunderstorm, or typhoon warning evidence.

official_source_candidates.name: CWA daily or event reports
official_source_candidates.use: Human-reviewed daily weather description for final event type labeling.

minimum_required_evidence: CWA weather map or daily report covering 2026-06-28 afternoon-evening
CWA warning/advisory check for Taiwan during the window
events.notes: Do not relabel this as front, Mei-yu, or typhoon-related until official context is attached.

minimum_required_evidence: CWA weather map or daily report covering 2026-07-02 afternoon
CWA warning/advisory check for Chiayi County during the window
events.notes: The radar peak is real locally, but the weather mechanism remains unconfirmed.

minimum_required_evidence: CWA weather map or daily report covering 2026-07-03 afternoon-evening
CWA warning/advisory check for Chiayi County during the window
events.notes: Use this as a radar candidate only until official context is attached.
